rows.title: What is the energy of the Relativistic Heavy Ion Collider (RHIC)
rows.prompt:
What is the energy of the Relativistic Heavy Ion Collider (RHIC) so that the speed of the nucleus X is equal to 0.96c?

Knowing that X is defined as Li with 3 neutrons.

PS: the precision of the energy is at 1e-4.

Choices:
A. 20.132 GeV
B. 18.475 GeV
C. 21.419
D. 23.069 GeV

Solve the question. At the end, write exactly one final line in this format and do not write anything after it:
Answer: <letter>

First, identify the nucleus X.
"Li with 3 neutrons" means Lithium with 3 neutrons.
Lithium (Li) has atomic number $Z = 3$.
The number of neutrons $N = 3$.
The mass number $A = Z + N = 3 + 3 = 6$.
So the nucleus is Lithium-6 ($^6\text{Li}$).

Next, I need to determine the rest mass of this nucleus.
The mass of a nucleus can be approximated by the sum of the masses of its protons and neutrons, or more accurately, using the atomic mass.
However, in high-energy physics problems, often the mass is approximated by $A \times m_p$ or using the atomic mass unit $u$.
